head.hi: Mr. William Phillips, Personal Representative of President Roosevelt in India, to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
New Delhi, February 26, 1943—6 p.m.
[Received February 26—3:26 p.m.]

div.p: 190. I have discussed with the Secretary for Foreign AffairsO. K. Caroe, Secretary to the Government of India in the External Affairs Department. the points raised in your 119, February 24, 10 p.m., which meet with my cordial approval.

Personally he sees no objection to designation of Ballantine and Williams as Counselors of Mission but will refer the matter to the Viceroy.At this time the Marquess of Linlithgow. He added that the latter might feel the necessity of obtaining approval from London.
